name: King's Hill - Hermitage of San Miquel - Pena Reservoir — circular from Beceite through the Sierra de Escanadé
name_original: Colina del Rey - Ermita de San Miquel - Embalse de Pena — circular desde Beceite por la Sierra de Escanadé
region_intro: Mountain biking around Beceite offers a diverse natural landscape characterized by rugged mountains, crystal-clear rivers, and dense forests. The region is dominated by the Puertos de Beceite mountain range, featuring steep terrain and deep canyons. Trails often wind through areas with almond and olive trees, alongside rivers like the Matarraña, providing varied riding experiences. This area provides a natural setting for mountain biking, with routes traversing through its distinctive geological formations. Best mountain bike trails around Beceite The most popular mountain bike trail is King's Hill - Hermitage of San Miquel - Pena Reservoir — circular from Beceite through the Sierra de Escanadé, a 26.6 miles (42.8 km) trail that takes 4 hours 18 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers panoramic views of the Sierra de Escanadé and the Pena Reservoir. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Estrechos de Arnés - Río de Algar - Toll de Vidre — desde Baceite por el Parque Natural dels Ports, a difficult 26.0 miles (41.9 km) path. This route traverses the Natural Park dels Ports, featuring river sections and natural pools. Local mountain bikers also love the Beceite – Estrets d'Arnes loop from la Botera, a 36.2 miles (58.3 km) trail leading through the dramatic Estrets d'Arnes canyon, often completed in about 5 hours 50 minutes. Mountain biking around Beceite is defined by rugged mountain ranges, river valleys, and dense forests, with trails winding through almond and olive groves. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The routes in Beceite are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 30 reviews. More than 300 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Beceite's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in Beceite?
A: There are nearly 30 mountain bike trails around Beceite, offering a diverse range of experiences for all skill levels. The komoot community has explored over 300 of these routes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Beceite?
A: Beceite's mountain bike trails traverse a rugged and diverse natural landscape. You'll encounter steep and challenging terrain in the Puertos de Beceite mountain range, winding paths through almond and olive groves, and scenic routes alongside rivers like the Matarraña. The area features deep canyons and varied geological formations, providing a dynamic riding experience.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners or families in Beceite?
A: Yes, Beceite offers options for various skill levels, including easy trails. For a scenic and less strenuous ride, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14888679" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Beceite – Pena Reservoir loop from Beceite / Beseit&lt;/a&gt;, which is classified as easy and covers about 19.8 km with moderate elevation gain.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the mountain bike trails?
A: Beceite's trails are rich with natural beauty. You can encounter stunning natural pools fed by rivers like the Matarraña, Ulldemó, and Pena. Notable features include the dramatic canyons of El Parrizal, and waterfalls such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2479086" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salt de Robert Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. The region is also home to the vast Puertos de Beceite mountain range, offering panoramic views.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Beceite?
A: Yes, many routes in Beceite are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. An example of a challenging circular route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995895809/king-s-hill-hermitage-of-san-miquel-pena-reservoir-circular-from-beceite-through-the-sierra-de-escanade" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;King's Hill - Hermitage of San Miquel - Pena Reservoir — circular from Beceite through the Sierra de Escanadé&lt;/a&gt;, which offers panoramic views of the Sierra de Escanadé.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Beceite?
A: The diverse landscape of Beceite can be enjoyed throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for mountain biking, with lush scenery in spring and vibrant colors in autumn. Summers can be hot, especially in the middle of the day, while winters are cooler but often still suitable for riding, particularly at lower elevations.

Q: What wildlife might I encounter while mountain biking in Beceite?
A: The Puertos de Beceite is a significant national hunting reserve, home to a rich variety of wildlife. You might spot Spanish ibex, golden eagles, griffon vultures, and wild boars. The tranquil environment offers a strong connection to nature, making wildlife sightings a possibility, especially in quieter areas.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Beceite?
A: The mountain bike trails in Beceite are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 30 reviews. Riders frequently praise the rugged mountain ranges, river valleys, and dense forests, as well as the unique experience of riding through almond and olive groves. The diverse terrain and stunning natural beauty are often highlighted.

Q: Are there any challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. Beceite is known for its steep and rugged terrain, offering numerous difficult routes for advanced riders. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38154377" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Beceite – Estrets d'Arnes loop from la Botera&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 58.3 km trail leading through the dramatic Estrets d'Arnes canyon, requiring very good fitness and advanced riding skills.

Q: Are there any natural pools or waterfalls accessible from the mountain bike trails?
A: Yes, Beceite is blessed with numerous rivers that feed over 50 natural pools, many of which are accessible from trails. Popular spots include L'Assut, Font de Rabosa (Zorro's Fountain), and Las Pesqueras. The rivers also carve through the landscape, creating picturesque scenes with waterfalls like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2479086" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Salt de La Portellada&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2479086" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salt de Robert Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I find accommodation or places to eat near the mountain bike trails?
A: Beceite is a village nestled in the Matarraña region, offering a tranquil escape. While specific amenities for bikers are not detailed, the village itself provides a base for exploring. The region's natural beauty and connection to nature suggest that local establishments would cater to visitors, though it's advisable to check for specific cafes or accommodation in advance.
tips: On this peak, known as the Hill of the Three Kings, three autonomous regions converge: Aragon, Catalonia and the Valencian Community, and legend has it that the three kings sat at this point to discuss their affairs. The views from here are beautiful.

On our way through the Hermitage of San Miquel I recommend stopping for a moment to enjoy this charming place with the remains of the Village and the Hermitage of San Miquel de Espinava. A quiet and beautiful place at the same time.
